page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate balanced performance across diverse workloads. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core performance falls within the average range. Memory performance is notable, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, though large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier. In application-specific tests, the server demonstrates strong image processing capabilities and strong text generation speeds for small language models. However, processing larger models, such as the 70B parameter model, results in weak performance, highlighting a clear tradeoff for large-scale AI tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32as_v5 provides a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale Premium Storage independently. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale text and image processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by a 3.525 GHz AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory scores, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. It performs well in image processing and small-model text generation, though it exhibits weak performance on large 70B parameter models. This server is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, and 128 GB of RAM, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and a standard one-to-four virtual CPU to memory ratio.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Supports Premium Storage with no bundled local disk storage
Suitable for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
